name: Nominated contacts
urlNote: Path segment is singular 'nominated-contact' (confirmed). Slug/filename retains plural 'nominated-contacts' as the internal page identifier. Evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d — GET/POST requests 300/316/1814/1830/2948/2964/4519/4535 all hit .../{chedReference}/nominated-contact, and every returnUrl param in the action-153 snapshot ends in '/nominated-contact'.
pageTitle: Nominated Contacts - Import and export applications - GOV.UK
caption: Contacts
heading: Nominated contacts (optional)
bodyCopy: Nominate up to 5 contacts. They will be notified if your consignment is chosen for inspection.

sourceCorroboration.pageObject: ipaffs-qa-automation/page-objects/notification/NominatedContactsPage.ts:1-9 — exposes ONLY btnSaveAndContinue (getByRole('button',{name:'Save and continue'})). No locators for Name/Email/Mobile inputs, 'Add another person' or 'Remove'. Corroborates the 'Save and continue' label (inferred); the multi-row / add / remove behaviour is entirely trace-derived and untested by QA.
sourceCorroboration.workflows: ched-p-workflows.ts:522-523, 794-795, 1001 — every CHED-P journey clicks straight through this page with NO field fills. ched-pp-workflows.ts and ched-d-workflows.ts do the same. DEFAULT_CONFIG (ched-p-workflows.ts:122) has no nominated-contacts field (the only 'contact' config keys — billing contact email/telephone, lines ~113/115 — belong to the separate billing/CUC page). Strongly corroborates that the whole page is optional and no field is required.
sourceCorroboration.accessibility: tests/accessibility/ched-p-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:244-246 — 'Nominated Contacts Page' appears in journey order between 'Contact Details Page' (line ~240) and 'Contact Address Page' (line ~248), then save-and-continue empty. Corroborates journey position (IPAFFS files IUU/fish as CHED-P, so the CHED-P journey is the applicable one).
sourceCorroboration.assertions: No test in tests/notification/ched-p/*.spec.ts asserts anything about this page (no expect on any field, message or max-rows rule). No error/validation copy is inferable from QA — the gap stands.

structure.text: Nominate up to 5 contacts. They will be notified if your consignment is chosen for inspection.
structure.govukComponent: Body (govuk-body, serves as fieldset hint via aria-describedby)

structure.text: Columns: Name / Email address / Mobile number / (visually-hidden) Remove contact details
structure.govukComponent: Table (govuk-table) used as a repeatable-row form layout
non-standard link-button (styled as a link, submits form)

govukComponents.usage: Wraps the whole contacts group; legend contains the caption + H1, hint bound via aria-describedby=nominated-contact-page-hint.
Hint/intro paragraph ('Nominate up to 5 contacts...') and the 'Cancel and return to hub' paragraph.
Repeatable contact rows laid out as a table (Name / Email address / Mobile number / Remove), with the text inputs embedded in cells.
Name (width-20), Email address (default width, type=email), Mobile number (width-20, type=tel). Labelled via aria-labelledby pointing at the table column headers, not a govuk-label.
Primary actions 'Save and return to hub' and 'Save and continue' (data-prevent-double-click).
'Cancel and return to hub' link to the notification overview/hub.
Accessible column header text 'Remove contact details' for the actions column.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Submit-type buttons styled to look like inline links, used for the row-level 'Remove' action and the 'Add another person' action. The add button also carries a bare 'govuk' class (no such utility exists — likely a template artefact).
nonStandardPatterns.concern: 'link-button' is a bespoke IPAFFS class, not a GOV.UK Design System class. GOV.UK guidance is that buttons should look like buttons; link-styled submit buttons are a custom pattern that the rebuild should not copy.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: GOV.UK 'Add another' pattern (govuk-frontend / hmrc add-to-a-list) with a secondary govuk-button for 'Add another' and a proper remove control per item; or a govuk-button--secondary.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Custom responsive-table wrapper class around the contacts table.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Not a govuk-* class; bespoke IPAFFS styling. The whole approach of embedding editable text inputs inside a data table is non-standard for GOV.UK forms — column headers double as input labels (aria-labelledby to <th>), so there are no per-field govuk-label elements or hints.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Replace the editable-table layout with the GOV.UK 'Add another' repeated-fieldset pattern: one fieldset per contact with proper govuk-label + govuk-hint for Name, Email address and Mobile number.

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: Text inputs labelled by aria-labelledby to table column headers (name-header / email-header / phone-header) instead of govuk-label
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Each row input borrows its accessible name from the column <th> rather than having a dedicated label element.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Field-level labels and hints are absent; accessibility relies on the table header association. Fine as a table but atypical for a GOV.UK form and loses per-field hint capacity.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Dedicated govuk-label (and optional govuk-hint) per input in an 'Add another' fieldset.

fields.name: name
fields.label: Name
fields.conditionalOn: one row per contact; 'Add another person' adds further rows (up to 5 contacts per body copy)
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 153 (input#name, aria-labelledby=name-header, autocomplete=name, govuk-input--width-20)
fields.corroboration: QA corroborates optional: every CHED-P workflow skips this page with no fill (ched-p-workflows.ts:522-523), and the page object exposes no Name locator (NominatedContactsPage.ts:1-9). No QA-inferable validation for this field.

fields.name: email
fields.label: Email address
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 153 (input#email type=email, aria-labelledby=email-header, autocomplete=email, spellcheck=false)
fields.corroboration: QA corroborates optional: skipped empty in all workflows; no Email locator in the page object; no test asserts an email-format validation message. Email-format rule is a gap for a human.

fields.name: telephone
fields.label: Mobile number
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 153 (input#telephone type=tel, aria-labelledby=phone-header, autocomplete=tel, govuk-input--width-20)
fields.corroboration: QA corroborates optional: skipped empty in all workflows; no Mobile/telephone locator in the page object; no test asserts a phone-format or mobile-only validation message. Format rule is a gap for a human.

validationMessages.message: gap — no validation error state observed. The page is optional and this trace saved-and-continued through it without entering any contacts.
validationMessages.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 153 (empty form, no error summary rendered). QA repo adds no evidence either way: no CHED-P test drives a field or asserts an error message on this page (page object exposes only Save and continue). Validation copy remains a genuine gap for a human.

openQuestions: The whole page is marked '(optional)' and no field has required=true; confirm whether any contact detail is conditionally mandatory once a partial row is started (e.g. name entered but email blank).
Body copy caps at 'up to 5 contacts' but the max-rows enforcement / disabling of 'Add another person' at 5 was not exercised in this trace — gap.
No inline field labels/hints exist (inputs borrow labels from table headers). Confirm intended field-level hint copy for the rebuild (e.g. email format, mobile number format).
'Mobile number' is the visible column/label but the input name/id is 'telephone' (type=tel) — confirm whether landline numbers are acceptable or mobile-only.
No back link was rendered in main on this page (navigation is via 'Cancel and return to hub' / 'Save and return to hub'); confirm the hub-return model is intended vs a standard GOV.UK back link.
This page was reached as an intermediate 'Save and continue' with empty fields; validation rules and error copy are unconfirmed (gap) — mine an errored trace if one exercises this page.

QA automation NEVER drives this page's fields — the page object exposes only 'Save and continue', DEFAULT_CONFIG has no nominated-contacts key, and every CHED-P/PP/D workflow clicks straight through empty. So the entire add-row / 'Add another person' / 'Remove' / max-5 behaviour is trace-derived and has zero test coverage. Confirm the rebuild's intended max-contacts cap and per-row partial-completion rules with a human, since no automated test pins them.

For the IUU rebuild: confirm whether a Nominated contacts page is even in scope. It carries no required data (optional, always skipped by tests) and appears mid-journey in the CHED-P flow (between Contact details and Contact address); a simpler service may fold or drop it.
